title: The Shattered Toast at Whitcombe's Table
openingIncident: At 8:45 pm, Eleanor Whitcombe, renowned art dealer, was found dead in a modest dining room table set with glass, bottle, plate and knife. Three people had been present during the final minutes, and each gave a different account. Nothing outside the room is needed to identify the killer.

suspects.name: Basil Harrington
suspects.statement: I left at 8:30 pm. I never touched the knife and did not go near the glass.
suspects.alibiClaim: I had already left the room by 8:30 pm and did not return.

suspects.name: Clara Pembroke
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, Nigel Ashcroft helped me set the bottle down on the side table. We left the room together immediately afterwards. Basil Harrington and Eleanor Whitcombe were still inside.
suspects.alibiClaim: Nigel Ashcroft and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

suspects.name: Nigel Ashcroft
suspects.statement: At 8:35 pm, I helped Clara Pembroke set the bottle down on the side table. We then left together and remained together until the body was found at 8:45 pm.
suspects.alibiClaim: Clara Pembroke and I were together outside the room from 8:35 pm until the discovery at 8:45 pm.

clues.title: The Edge of Knife
clues.detail: A fresh line of blood runs along one edge of the knife, and its point has a new bend. Eleanor Whitcombe has a narrow wound consistent with that edge.

clues.title: Ring beneath Bottle
clues.detail: The fresh circular ring beneath the bottle is crossed by a narrow blood droplet from the attack whose edge remains unbroken. Clara Pembroke and Nigel Ashcroft both state that they set the bottle down on the side table at 8:35 pm before leaving together.

clues.title: Fibres on Knife
clues.detail: Fine copper-coloured silk threads are caught around the handle of the knife. Basil Harrington's copper silk pocket square has a fresh narrow tear, exposing the same alternating copper and navy stripe.

clues.title: Fragment at Glass
clues.detail: A curved black lacquer chip edged in gold is lodged inside the handle joint of the glass on top of a narrow blood droplet from the attack. The black-and-gold fountain-pen cap worn by Basil Harrington has a fresh curved gap in the lacquer; the exposed edges visibly fit the fragment.

clues.title: Crack in Plate
clues.detail: A crack runs across the plate. A continuous yellowed glue line is visible deep inside the break.

solution.motive: Basil Harrington killed Eleanor Whitcombe because the threat of exposing his embezzlement scheme.
solution.method: Eleanor Whitcombe was fatally stabbed with the knife.
solution.timeOfDeath: After 8:35 pm and before discovery at 8:45 pm
solution.opportunity: Basil Harrington claimed to have left at 8:30 pm. The timeline evidence places the attack after Clara Pembroke and Nigel Ashcroft left together at 8:35 pm; the fine copper-coloured silk threads on the knife and the black and gold fragment recovered from the glass place Basil Harrington inside afterwards. Basil Harrington therefore had the only evidenced opportunity to be alone with Eleanor Whitcombe and reach the knife during the murder window.
